source.definition: to construct or create something by putting parts or materials together

explanation.sourceText: The English word "build" originates from Old English "byldan", meaning to construct or erect. It is related to similar words in other Germanic languages and has evolved to mean the act of making or assembling something.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „build“ provine din engleza veche „byldan“, care înseamnă a construi sau a ridica. Este legat de cuvinte similare din alte limbi germanice și a evoluat pentru a desemna acțiunea de a face sau asambla ceva.

source.additional: Commonly used in contexts involving construction of buildings, structures, or other physical objects.

target.definition: a crea sau a produce ceva nou

explanation.sourceText: Derived from the Latin verb "construere" (to build together), "a construi" in Romanian developed as a regular verb for building and creating something new.
explanation.targetText: Derivat din verbul latin „construere“ (a construi împreună), „a construi“ în română desemnează acțiunea de a crea sau a ridica ceva nou.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 4th conjugation (-i). Frequently used in architectural and engineering contexts.

examples.sourceText: Are you building the office building?
The architect builds a modern building.
We build a school in the city.
examples.targetText: Noi construim o școală în oraș.

examples.sourceText: Are you building the new sports center?

examples.targetText: Ei construiesc mai multe case de locuit.

examples.sourceText: I have built the new building.
They built the bridge last week.
He built many houses in his career.
examples.targetText: El construia multe case în cariera sa.

examples.sourceText: I had already built the building before the plans were approved.
examples.targetText: Construisem deja clădirea înainte să fie aprobate planurile.

examples.sourceText: I will build a new shopping center.
examples.targetText: Voi construi un nou centru comercial.

examples.sourceText: He will build the new school soon.
examples.targetText: El o să construiască noua școală în curând.

examples.sourceText: I would build the building if I had the permits.
examples.targetText: Aș construi clădirea, dacă aș avea avizele.

examples.sourceText: It is necessary that you build the house by next month.
examples.targetText: Este necesar să construiești casa până luna viitoare.

examples.sourceText: The constructed building has stood here for years.
examples.targetText: Clădirea construită stă aici de ani de zile.

examples.sourceText: While I was building the site, I also planted trees.
examples.targetText: Construind terenul, am plantat și copaci.

learningTips.text: Remember the English word "build" sounds like "bild" in German, but think of it as 'to put together'—just like assembling blocks to create something new, which matches the Romanian "a construi".
The prefix "con-" in Romanian "construi" means 'together', so imagine building something by joining parts, similar to the English concept of construction.
Focus on the present tense forms: "construiesc" (I build) and "construiești" (you build) to help you conjugate the verb correctly in Romanian.
Visualize a construction project: "I build" = "Eu construiesc" — linking the meaning and sound will help you remember the Romanian verb.
